stay_areas.why: Easy MTR access, walkable food/harbour vibes.

transit_notes: Airport Express vs bus depending on hotel
city_briefs.nasha_hook: Street seafood + dim sum city. Easy to do local spots + one nicer harbour meal without making it formal.
seafood_street_hits: Temple Street / Jordan: claypot + seafood stir-fries (go where it’s busy).
Sham Shui Po: fish ball noodles / cheung fun stalls (local vibe).
Typhoon shelter style crab/shrimp (street-to-sitdown depending on place).
city_briefs.nice_sitdown_pick: One harbour-view Cantonese seafood set (keep it simple: steamed fish + prawns + veg).
city_briefs.signature_seafood: Typhoon-shelter crab night (or a steamed fish + prawns harbour meal).

stay_areas.why: Good for day trips + train connections.

transit_notes: JR Kansai–Hiroshima pass for Hiroshima leg
city_briefs.nasha_hook: Kuromon Market for seafood street snacking + late-night izakaya plates. Very “eat as you walk”.
seafood_street_hits: Kuromon Ichiba: grilled scallops, uni, oysters (sample-and-go).
Dotonbori street food loop: takoyaki + seafood skewers.
Conveyor-belt sushi for quick, fun, cheap tasting.
city_briefs.nice_sitdown_pick: One proper sushi/kaisendon dinner (reserve if it’s a famous place).
city_briefs.signature_seafood: Kuromon Market scallop/uni crawl + sushi dinner.

transit_notes: JR ferry to Miyajima covered by pass (if using JR ferry)
city_briefs.nasha_hook: This is the Japan “oyster chapter”. We lean into it hard.
seafood_street_hits: Hiroshima oysters: grilled/fried/raw depending on comfort level.
Miyajima: anago (conger eel) + oysters near the ferry area.
city_briefs.nice_sitdown_pick: Dedicated oyster dinner (sitdown) — make this the “signature meal” of Japan leg.
city_briefs.signature_seafood: Oysters in Hiroshima + Miyajima anago (eel) lunch.

city_briefs.nasha_hook: Korea’s seafood playground: market wow-factor + coastal dinner photos.
seafood_street_hits: Jagalchi Fish Market: pick seafood + eat it fresh (iconic).
Eomuk (Busan fish cake) snacks for casual street bites.
city_briefs.nice_sitdown_pick: One seaside set meal (seafood hotpot / grilled shellfish) — go for vibes + comfort.

coastal_options.why: Best “coastal holiday” feel without leaving Busan; easy logistics.
More local coastline + seafood; could be cheaper and very food-forward.
Bigger “seafood town / island coast” energy, but more travel time.

city_briefs.signature_seafood: Jagalchi market pick-your-seafood + seaside grilled clams (Haeundae/Cheongsapo).

transit_notes: Consider renting a car vs taxis depending on plan
city_briefs.nasha_hook: Jeju is the “island food” leg: abalone + seafood soups + coastal cafes.
seafood_street_hits: Seafood hotpot / stews (comfort food after exploring).
Seaside grilled cuttlefish / shellfish stands (simple + local).
city_briefs.nice_sitdown_pick: One black pork BBQ + seafood sides night (Jeju signature).
city_briefs.signature_seafood: Abalone porridge breakfast + seafood hotpot night (island vibes).
